channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa expected to rapidly intensify into hurricane on Saturday
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Tropical Storm Melissa is expected to rapidly intensify into a hurricane on Saturday, the National Hurricane Center said. The storm is currently 165 miles southeast of Kingston, Jamaica, and around 240 miles southwest of Port Au Prince, Haiti.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa poised to become a hurricane and drop torrential rain on Caribbean
item.description: KINGSTON, Jamaica (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa was expected to strengthen into a hurricane on Saturday as forecasters warned of massive rainfall and life-threatening flooding and landslides in the northern Caribbean, with an astounding 35 inches (89 centimeters) of rain anticipated in southwest Hai
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Anti-abortion pregnancy centers are looking to offer much more than ultrasounds and diapers
item.description: More anti-abortion pregnancy centers have been offering medical services such as testing for sexually transmitted infections.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill And Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Early voting begins in New York City mayor's race and contest to pick New Jersey's next governor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Polling places open Saturday for the start of in-person voting for two of the year's most closely watched elections: The New York City mayor's race and the contest to pick New Jersey's next governor.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump speaks at tele-rally to boost GOP's chances in governor's race in New Jersey
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump urged his supporters in New Jersey late Friday to cast a ballot for the Republican candidate for governor, Jack Ciattarelli, before early voting starts in the state on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Shots fired, driver hurt and held after truck rams into Coast Guard base in California
item.description: A truck driver who backed into a U.S. Coast Guard base in the San Francisco Bay Area — the site of earlier protests against federal immigration agents — was shot by law enforcement officers and wounded, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart is changing how hourly store workers earn raises
item.description: The retail giant's new approach will take employee performance into account as well as years of service.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: Bill to fund SNAP in shutdown introduced in House
item.description: Rep. Mariannette Miller-Meeks (R-Iowa) on Friday introduced a bill to fund the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) as the government shutdown threatens payments of the food aid in some states.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Missing California girl seen in security image potentially wearing wig to hide identity
item.description: New images released by the Santa Barbara County Sheriff's Office on Friday shows a girl who is believed to be missing 9-year-old Melodee Buzzard wearing a wig at a car rental business prior to leaving the state with her mother.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Experts say criminal intent will be key in prosecuting NBA's Billups and Rozier in gambling cases
item.description: Gambling is at the center of two blockbuster indictments targeting NBA coach Chauncey Billups and Miami Heat player Terry Rozier.
item.dc:creator: Ed White And John Seewer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Officials investigating after deadly crane collapse in commercial area north of Boston
item.description: Authorities, including officials from OSHA, are investigating after a fatal crane collapse at a commercial site north of Boston in Everett, Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 7 charged in 2024 Pennsylvania voter registration fraud that prosecutors say was motivated by money
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A yearlong investigation into suspected fraudulent voter registration forms submitted ahead of last year's presidential election produced criminal charges Friday against six street canvassers and the man who led their work in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US says it now plans to deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia as soon as Oct. 31
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — The U.S. government plans to deport Kilmar Abrego Garcia to Liberia, and could do so as early as Oct. 31, according to a Friday court filing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: In emotional speech, Zohran Mamdani defends Muslim identity against 'racist and baseless' attacks
item.description: Zohran Mamdani, the Democratic nominee for New York City mayor, is pledging to embrace his Muslim identity amid growing attacks in the final days of the campaign.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cholesterol medication recalled over 'failed dissolution specifications': What to know
item.description: “In some instances, stopping your medicine may be more harmful to your health than continuing to take the recalled medicine,” the FDA says.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Judge halts death penalty case in New Mexico due to federal shutdown
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A judge in New Mexico has halted a federal death penalty case, citing the pinch of the U.S. government shutdown and a funding shortfall for specialized public defenders that stretches back to the summer.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mafia returns to center stage in NBA betting scandal that included rigged poker games
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Decades after a crackdown by prosecutors decimated the ranks of the New York Mafia, the indictment of an NBA coach, a player and nearly three dozen others in a betting scandal highlighted the mob's persistence and adaptability to changing times and technology.
item.dc:creator: ADAM GELLER and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poll: California's Prop 50 likely to pass, Republican surges in governor's race
item.description: With less than two weeks before election day, California voters appear poised to pass the state’s redistricting measure, Proposition 50, to counter Republican redistricting efforts in Texas to gain more seats in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WGN 9

item.title: Man shot 10 times protecting high school cheerleader who died after shooting at bonfire: report
item.description: An Alabama man was shot 10 times while trying to protect a high school cheerleader at a bonfire on early Sunday, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NFL seeks dismissal of Jon Gruden's lawsuit after Nevada Supreme Court sends it back to state court
item.description: The Nevada Supreme Court returned Jon Gruden’s case to the state’s District Court on Thursday, and the NFL filed two motions seeking the prompt dismissal of the claims.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI AP Pro Football Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Louisiana man arrested in child porn investigation
item.description: A 21-year-old Shreveport man was arrested and faces ten charges related to the possession of juvenile pornography after a search warrant was executed at his residence.
item.dc:creator: Erykah Agers, NewsNation

item.title: Chemists in Ohio warn of rising carfentanil cases as drug mixtures grow more dangerous
item.description: Authorities across the U.S. are warning of carfentanil, a dangerous synthetic opioid 10,000 times more potent than morphine and 100 times stronger than fentanyl.
item.dc:creator: Savannah Young, WGN 9

item.title: Here’s how indictment says Terry Rozier shared information that paid off for bettors
item.description: NBA guard Terry Rozier was averaging nearly 21 points per game for the Charlotte Hornets as the 2022-23 season neared an end. But during a game against the New Orleans Pelicans, he scored only 5 and played less than 10 minutes before claiming he was hurt.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: States worry about how to fill the gap in food aid ahead of a federal benefits halt
item.description: Officials in Louisiana, Vermont and Virginia pledged Thursday to keep food aid flowing to recipients in their states, even if the federal program is stalled next month because of the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: SNAP benefits may run out Nov. 1. Here's how you could still get food assistance
item.description: A disruption or delay could impact 42 million people, or 1 in 8 Americans, who rely on SNAP to buy food.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Florida crocodile found with spear, bullet in its head; Zoo works to save her
item.description: An 8-foot American crocodile is on her way to recovery after what appears to have been a harrowing ordeal.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cracker Barrel CEO explains reason for attempt to change logo: report
item.description: Cracker Barrel had debuted its new logo in August, but quickly faced backlash for ditching a depiction of the “Old Timer” previously seen on the sign.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Entire White House East Wing now demolished as Trump moves forward with ballroom construction
item.description: The entire White House East Wing has been demolished as President Donald Trump moves forward with a ballroom construction, Associated Press photos on Thursday showed.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White House releases donor list for Trump ballroom amid East Wing demolition
item.description: Amazon, Apple, Google and other major companies are among those donating to help cover the cost of President Trump's massive new ballroom at the White House. The White House confirmed the list to The Hill amid demolition of the East Wing to make room for the ballroom.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trail Blazers’ Billups, Heat’s Rozier among dozens charged in Mafia-linked gambling probe
item.description: Portland Trail Blazers head coach Chauncey Billups and Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier are among more than 30 people who have been charged in connection with schemes involving illegal sports betting and rigged poker games backed by Mafia, authorities said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: ICE looking to hire over 40 health care workers
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security is hiring for more than 40 medical positions in its immigration detention centers across the nation.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation, Fox 5

item.title: Amazon hopes robots can replace 600K future hires: Report
item.description: Amazon hopes robots can replace hundreds of thousands of future roles, according to leaked documents seen by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: Trump says he's owed a 'lot of money' over federal probes. Here's how the government could pay him
item.description: The potential that the president might take taxpayer money from the same government he leads has raised numerous ethical questions, especially since Trump has made cutting federal spending a top administration priority.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Unexpected colon cancer screening test show up at your door? This may be why
item.description: Did you receive a Cologuard test at your door, despite not ordering one? Someone may have ordered it for you.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Parties agree to extension banning deployment of National Guard troops to Illinois, but Supreme Court decision looms
item.description: CHICAGO (AP/WGN) — A federal judge in Chicago has agreed to extend a temporary restraining order banning the deployment of National Guard troops to Illinois by 30 days, hours before a temporary restraining order blocking the deployment was set to expire. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Lawsuit seeks to force swearing in of US Rep.-elect Adelita Grijalva of Arizona
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Arizona Attorney General Kris Mayes has filed a lawsuit that seeks to get Democrat Adelita Grijalva sworn in as the state’s newest member of Congress after U.S. House Speaker Mike Johnson has refused to seat her a month since winning the post.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House begins demolishing part of East Wing for Trump ballroom
item.description: Demolition crews appeared to start tearing down part of the White House East Wing to begin construction on President Trump’s planned ballroom.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5
